name: Climb to the Sanctuary of San Miguel de Aralar — circular from Alsasua
name_original: Subida al Santuario de San Miguel de Aralar — circular desde Alsasua
region_intro: Road cycling routes around San Millán / Donemiliaga traverse a diverse landscape in the historical territory of Álava, characterized by rural areas, mountains, and flatter plains. The region offers a mix of terrain, from gentle climbs around the Llanada Alavesa to more challenging ascents towards significant peaks and mountain passes. Cyclists can explore routes that pass through natural monuments and scenic areas, providing varied experiences for different skill levels. Best road cycling routes around San Millán / Donemiliaga The most popular road cycling route is Climb to the Sanctuary of San Miguel de Aralar — circular from Alsasua, a 57.5 miles (92.5 km) trail that takes 6 hours 37 minutes to complete. This difficult route features significant elevation gain towards the Sanctuary of San Miguel de Aralar. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is Zalduondo – Agurain/Salvatierra loop from Araia, an easy 23.4 miles (37.6 km) path. This loop offers a gentle ride through the rural landscapes of the Llanada Alavesa. Local road cyclists also love the Fields of the Llanada Alavesa – Bar Urbasa loop from Araia, a 37.7 miles (60.6 km) trail leading through open fields and rural scenery, often completed in about 2 hours 35 minutes. Road cycling around San Millán / Donemiliaga is defined by rural plains, rolling hills, and mountain ascents, offering options for various ability levels. The routes in San Millán / Donemiliaga are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.5 stars from more than 20 reviews. More than 3000 road cyclists have used komoot to explore San Millán / Donemiliaga's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many road cycling routes are available in San Millán / Donemiliaga?
A: There are over 10 dedicated road cycling routes around San Millán / Donemiliaga, offering a variety of experiences for different skill levels. These routes are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.5 stars.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on road cycling routes in San Millán / Donemiliaga?
A: The region offers a diverse landscape, from the rural plains of the Llanada Alavesa to rolling hills and more challenging mountain ascents towards significant peaks and mountain passes. You'll find a mix of terrain, including flatter areas, especially around Vitoria-Gasteiz, and scenic routes passing natural monuments.

Q: Are there road cycling routes suitable for beginners or families?
A: Yes, San Millán / Donemiliaga offers options for all abilities. For an easier ride, consider routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38219285" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Zalduondo – Agurain/Salvatierra loop from Araia&lt;/a&gt;, which is an easy 23.4-mile path through rural landscapes. The Ullíbarri-Gamboa Reservoir also offers a popular circular route with gentle climbs, suitable for families.

Q: What are some challenging road cycling routes for experienced riders?
A: For experienced cyclists seeking a challenge, routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1035231282/climb-to-the-sanctuary-of-san-miguel-de-aralar-circular-from-alsasua" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Climb to the Sanctuary of San Miguel de Aralar — circular from Alsasua&lt;/a&gt; offer significant elevation gain over 57.5 miles. Another demanding option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40323750" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Urbasa Hairpin Bends – Alto de Iturrieta (990 m) loop from Araia&lt;/a&gt;, which covers over 68 miles with substantial climbing.

Q: Are there any circular road cycling routes in the area?
A: Yes, many routes in San Millán / Donemiliaga are designed as loops. Examples include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38219285" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Zalduondo – Agurain/Salvatierra loop from Araia&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/32107533" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Fields of the Llanada Alavesa – Bar Urbasa loop from Araia&lt;/a&gt;. The Ullíbarri-Gamboa Reservoir also features a popular circular route.

Q: What historical or cultural points of interest can I visit while road cycling?
A: Cyclists can incorporate visits to historical sites such as the 12th-century San Bernardo de Barria monastery, now a hostel, or the well-preserved Eguilaz (Aizkomendi) dolmen. You might also pass by the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2989133" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sanctuary of Our Lady of Estíbaliz&lt;/a&gt; or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4857424" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Arantzazu Sanctuary&lt;/a&gt;, depending on your chosen route.

Q: Are there any natural attractions or viewpoints along the road cycling routes?
A: Yes, the region is rich in natural beauty. Routes often pass by tranquil lakes, scenic mountain passes, and natural monuments. You can also find highlights like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1630139" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Vasco-Navarro Railway Greenway&lt;/a&gt;, offering peaceful cycling through serene environments, or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/837300" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;San Adrián Tunnel&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Can I find waterfalls or rivers on road cycling routes in San Millán / Donemiliaga?
A: While road cycling routes primarily focus on paved roads, some areas are close to natural water features. You can find highlights like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3080140" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Tobería Waterfalls&lt;/a&gt;, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5027459" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Source of the Zirauntza River&lt;/a&gt;, or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2867152" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Igoroin Waterfalls&lt;/a&gt; in the broader region, which might be accessible via short detours or nearby paths.

Q: What is the best time of year for road cycling in San Millán / Donemiliaga?
A: The diverse landscape of San Millán / Donemiliaga makes it suitable for cycling through many seasons. Spring and autumn generally offer pleasant temperatures and vibrant scenery. Summer is also popular, especially for higher altitude routes, while the lower plains can be enjoyed during milder periods.

Q: Is there public transport available for cyclists in the San Millán / Donemiliaga area?
A: The broader Basque Country, including areas around San Millán / Donemiliaga, has a strong cycling culture and infrastructure. While specific public transport options for bikes vary, the region is well-equipped with greenways and cycle paths, often connecting towns and making it easier to access routes without a car.

Q: Are there places to eat or drink along the road cycling routes?
A: Many routes pass through charming villages where you can find local cafes and restaurants. The Basque Country is known for its gastronomy, so you'll likely encounter opportunities to refuel and experience local culture along your ride.

Q: What do other road cyclists enjoy most about cycling in San Millán / Donemiliaga?
A: The area is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 20 reviews. Cyclists often praise the varied terrain, from the peaceful rural plains to the challenging mountain ascents, and the opportunity to combine cycling with visits to historical sites and natural beauty.

Located between the Aralar mountains, it is a renowned spiritual center and one of the oldest in Navarre. Its 12th century Romanesque architecture is an architectural jewel worth visiting and admiring.
